column1: Home loan rates — salaried vs self-employed · June 2026
Salaried borrowers typically get 0.05%–0.25% lower rates than self-employed due to income stability. Self-employed rates rise further for sub-750 CIBIL profiles. RBI repo: 5.25 % (Apr 2026).
Sources: Urban Money (Jun 2026) · Business Standard / Paisabazaar (Jan 2026) · Union Bank official rate sheet (Apr 2026)

† SE rates for lenders that do not publish separate SE slabs are estimated at +0.10–0.25% over salaried for banks, and +0.25–0.50% for HFCs/NBFCs/SFBs, consistent with industry norms. CIBIL band spreads for lenders without public slab disclosures are estimated from published ranges and RBI data. Always get a personalised quote before applying.
